articles.title: Gas and Oil Prices Go Up as Shipping Lane in Danger
articles.summary: Gas and oil prices are going up fast, and stock markets are falling because of a problem in a key shipping lane called the Strait of Hormuz. An Iranian official said they might stop ships from passing through, which is worrying because about 20% of the world's oil and gas goes through there. In the UK, gas prices jumped by over 46%, and oil prices rose too. Stock markets in the UK, France, and Germany dropped. This conflict could make things more expensive for people, like energy bills and food, and might affect interest rates. It reminds people of when Russia invaded Ukraine, which also caused high prices. Higher prices could hurt the global economy, so investors are watching closely.

articles.title: School Talks Compared Student Loans to Phone Bills
articles.summary: A long time ago, the government gave talks in schools to tell teenagers about student loans. They compared paying back student loans to paying for a phone contract, which costs about £30 a month. The talks were given by graduates who worked for the government. They were told not to use words like 'debt' to make the loans seem less scary. Some people now say this was misleading because student loans can be hard to pay back. The government today says they are trying to make the loan system fairer. Student loans help people go to university, but it's important to understand how much money you will need to pay back later.

articles.title: Supreme Court Lets Schools Tell Parents About Transgender Students
articles.summary: The Supreme Court made a decision that allows schools in California to tell parents if their children say they are transgender, even if the children do not want their parents to know. This happened because some parents and teachers, who are part of a religious group, asked the court to stop a California law. That law said schools could not automatically tell parents about changes in their child's gender identity at school. The court agreed with the parents, saying their religious beliefs are important. But other judges disagreed, thinking the court should wait. This case is about balancing children's privacy and parents' rights. Schools now have to decide what to do while the case continues in lower courts.

articles.title: President Trump Will Go to a Big Dinner with Journalists
articles.summary: President Donald Trump says he will go to the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 25. This is a special event where the president meets with journalists. Trump has never gone to this dinner before as president. He was invited every year but did not attend. He said he did not go because he felt the press was not nice to him. Now, he has accepted the invitation and looks forward to it. The dinner started in 1921, and President Calvin Coolidge was the first president to go. Most presidents have attended, but some like Jimmy Carter and Richard Nixon did not go every year. The dinner was canceled in 2020 and 2021 because of the coronavirus. President Joe Biden went to the dinner during his time. Trump's decision to go this year is a big change.

articles.title: Supreme Court Keeps a Republican District in New York City
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, which is the highest court in the United States, made a decision to keep a congressional district in New York City that is held by a Republican politician. This district is the only one in the city where Republicans have a seat. A lower court had said the district was unfair to Black and Hispanic voters and needed to be redrawn, but the Supreme Court stopped that order. The court's conservative judges agreed with Republicans, while the liberal judges disagreed. This decision helps Republicans keep control of the district for the 2026 elections. The case is about how voting maps are drawn and who gets to vote in elections. It shows a big fight between political parties over power in the government.

articles.title: A Reporter's Story from Tehran During Attacks
articles.summary: A reporter named Reza Sayah is in Tehran, Iran, and he tells us what is happening there. For three days, the U.S. and Israel have been attacking Iran with airstrikes. Reza hears loud jet fighters and big explosions that shake the ground. The attacks started at midnight and lasted for 90 minutes. The Internet was cut off, so Reza cannot use it to send messages. He talks on the phone instead. The strikes are hitting military buildings, but also non-military places like hospitals, schools, and homes. Reza visited a police station that was destroyed, and nearby houses were damaged too. He met a man in a cafe who saw a scary video of someone hurt during an attack. The man described two explosions close together. U.S. President Trump said Iran wants to talk, but he also warned of more strikes. The situation is very tense and dangerous for people in Tehran.
